summary: The IDF destroyed a Hezbollah underground terror infrastructure in the village of Majd al-Zun in southern Lebanon, a tunnel over 200 meters long and more than 25 meters deep containing hundreds of weapons and launch shafts intended to strike Israeli territory. Iran and the US agreed to halt attacks on each other and will meet in Doha on Tuesday to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, following intense US military airlift activity in the region over the past seven days. Hezbollah targeted an Israeli field command headquarters in southern Lebanon with an IED, wounding two reserve soldiers, one seriously, and also struck a command post in a retaliatory attack.
key_points: IDF destroyed a Hezbollah underground terror infrastructure in Majd al-Zun, southern Lebanon, a tunnel over 200 meters long and more than 25 meters deep containing hundreds of weapons and launch shafts aimed at Israeli territory. (Source: @almogboker78/17625)
Iran and the US agreed to stop attacking each other and plan to meet on Tuesday in Doha to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @shin_persian/7289)

IDF and ISA eliminated terrorist Zarah Barham Abu Salem of the PIJ, who infiltrated Israel on October 7 and participated in abducting Israeli civilians from their homes. (Source: @New_security8200/202147)

Hezbollah targeted an Israeli field command headquarters in southern Lebanon with an explosive device, wounding two reserve soldiers, one in serious condition and one in moderate condition, evacuated by helicopter. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373548)

IDF struck three Hezbollah headquarters in the areas of Nabatieh and Mayfadoun in southern Lebanon in response to continued attacks on Israeli forces in the security zone. (Source: @idf_telegram/24467)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi and Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi arrived in Oman to discuss joint control of the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @asafroz/50835)

IDF Golani Brigade forces encountered a Hezbollah terrorist in the Dir Siran area of southern Lebanon, resulting in the death of Sgt. David Hazut and one lightly wounded soldier. (Source: @idf_telegram/24465)
IRGC Navy warned vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz that they must not transit without authorization and must use only the route designated by Iran. (Source: @presstv/196101)

sections.executive_summary: The IDF destroyed a major Hezbollah underground terror infrastructure in Majd al-Zun, southern Lebanon, a tunnel over 200 meters long and more than 25 meters deep containing hundreds of weapons and launch shafts aimed at Israeli territory. Iran and the US agreed to halt attacks on each other and will meet in Doha on Tuesday to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, following intense US military airlift activity in the region over the past seven days. Hezbollah continued targeting Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, wounding two reserve soldiers in an IED attack on a field command headquarters.

points: Iran and the US agreed to stop attacking each other and plan to meet on Tuesday in Doha to resolve their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, according to Axios. (Source: @shin_persian/7289)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi and Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi arrived in Oman to discuss joint control of the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @asafroz/50835)

IRGC Navy warned vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz that they must not transit without authorization and must use only the route designated by Iran, with violators bearing full responsibility. (Source: @presstv/196101)

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian announced that $6 billion of Iran's $12 billion in funds in Qatar has been released and returned to the country, with efforts ongoing to recover the remaining amount. (Source: @VahidOnline/76740)
Numerous aircraft with transponders turned off were observed flying over Karaj and western Tehran at cruising altitude. (Source: @shin_persian/7275)

Former head of Iran's Atomic Energy Organization Ali Akbar Salehli stated that Iran's 60% uranium enrichment was initiated for peaceful purposes, specifically to produce radio-drugs (molybdenum) similar to Belgium's process. (Source: @SharghDaily/168759)

IDF Chief of Staff Eil Amir stated at a 1,000-day war commemoration that battlefield leadership and fighter bravery led the IDF to victory across all sectors. (Source: @yediotnews25/132046)

IDF probe found that a June 19 Hezbollah attack that killed a tank battalion commander and three soldiers was carried out by an explosive-laden drone, not an anti-tank guided missile. (Source: @manniefabian/58698)

Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar addressed the parliamentary lobby for peace and normalization with Lebanon, stating the goal is peace conditioned on border security and ending Iranian occupation of Lebanon through Hezbollah. (Source: @N12chat/215158)

IDF forces in the Southern Command remain deployed in the area in accordance with the agreement and will continue to remove any threat. (Source: @New_security8200/202147)

IDF released a new 'Lavia' helicopter with more horsepower, higher moment, and a new turbo system, now integrated into the 810th Brigade's special unit and already operating in Syria and Lebanon. (Source: @idf_telegram/24470)

Israel notified the US and the American representative in Lebanon in advance. (Source: @almogboker78/17625)

sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo in southern Lebanon remains high, with Hezbollah continuing to target Israeli forces in the security zone through IEDs and retaliatory strikes on command posts, while the IDF conducts targeted strikes on Hezbollah headquarters and underground infrastructure. The Strait of Hormuz dispute between Iran and the US represents a significant escalation vector, though the agreement to halt attacks and meet in Doha suggests a potential de-escalation pathway. The IRGC Navy's warning to vessels indicates Iran is asserting control over the strait, which could threaten global energy flows. The linking of the Lebanese and Iranian arenas, as acknowledged by Israeli Defense Minister Katz, suggests Hezbollah operations may be increasingly coordinated with Iranian strategic objectives.
sections.regional_response: Oman is hosting Iranian diplomatic leadership for discussions on joint control of the Strait of Hormuz, signaling Gulf states' involvement in the maritime dispute. Qatar is serving as the venue for US-Iran negotiations, with the White House confirming Witkoff and Kushner's attendance. Israel notified the US and the American representative in Lebanon in advance of the Majd al-Zun tunnel destruction, indicating continued coordination with Washington despite reported US pressure to limit strikes on Beirut. Israeli Foreign Minister Saar is actively pursuing a parliamentary lobby for peace and normalization with Lebanon, conditioned on border security and ending Iranian occupation through Hezbollah.
sections.intelligence_notes: The IDF probe revealing that a June 19 Hezbollah attack was carried out by an explosive-laden drone rather than an anti-tank guided missile indicates Hezbollah is employing evolving unmanned aerial tactics against Israeli armor. The observation of numerous aircraft with transponders off over Karaj and western Tehran suggests covert aerial activity in Iran, though the nature and origin remain unconfirmed. Western intelligence sources indicate the Houthis are exploiting the current period to improve their missile capabilities through testing, likely targeting range and accuracy improvements. The cancellation of this week's Switzerland talks due to new hostilities suggests diplomatic channels remain fragile and subject to disruption by battlefield developments.
